title: dsconfig v2 Extensions
description: Formal JSON Schema describing the wire format of a SchemaV2Extensions side-table document, as implemented by SCHEMA-V2.go and SCHEMA-V2.ts. IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: this document describes v2's new capabilities (scopes, role, roleConflicts, pairRole) as a SEPARATE document, keyed by field id, that is paired with — but does not modify the shape of — a v1 dsconfig Schema document validated against https://grafana.com/schemas/dsconfig/v1/SCHEMA-V1.json. This separation exists only because this reference implementation was produced under a constraint of not modifying the v1 SCHEMA-V1.go/SCHEMA-V1.ts/SCHEMA-V1.json files. The proposed upstream target shape, described in full in GRF-RFC-0043, is for these same properties (scopes, role, roleConflicts, pairRole) to instead become four new, optional properties directly on a v1 configField object — at which point this entire file would be deleted and its $defs merged directly into SCHEMA-V1.json's existing configField definition. Treat this file as a faithful, behavior-preserving stand-in for that target shape, not as the final proposed format.

scopeDef.description: Declares one named scope a multi-connection plugin's fields may belong to — for example, a plugin with a 'controller' API and a separate 'eum' API declares two scopeDefs, one per connection.

id.description: This scope's unique identifier within the schema document. Referenced by fieldExtensionV2.scopes.

label.description: Human-readable name for this scope, for UI and documentation purposes (e.g. 'Controller API').

role.description: A field's semantic meaning, independent of its key or id, drawn from a fixed, versioned vocabulary. A role outside this enum is rejected by both reference implementations' v2 validation (SCHEMA-V2.go's ValidateV2, SCHEMA-V2.ts's validateV2); a JSON-Schema-only validator checking a fieldExtensionV2 against this $def enforces the same closed vocabulary.

pairRole.description: For an item field used inside an indexedPair-mapped field's item.fields, declares whether that item field is the pair's 'key' (name) half or its 'value' half. Absence (this property not set on a fieldExtensionV2) means positional inference applies, exactly as under v1 — the first declared item field is treated as the name, the second as the value.

fieldExtensionV2.description: One field's v2-only metadata, keyed by that field's id in the companion schemaV2Extensions.fields map. Every property is optional; an entry with every property absent is equivalent to having no entry at all.

scopes.description: This field's scope membership. Empty/absent means the field is shared across every scope declared in the owning schemaV2Extensions.scopeDefs, including any scope declared later in a future revision of the same schema. A non-empty array must list fewer than every currently-declared scope id — listing all of them is rejected by both reference implementations' validation, specifically because it would NOT extend to a scope added later the way omission does, and allowing both spellings of 'all scopes' to coexist invites silent divergence between them.

roleConflicts.description: Roles that must not be simultaneously active alongside this field's own role. Each entry must be a known role value; a field must not list its own role as a conflict. This is checked structurally (every named role exists, and no two fields in the same effective scope carry conflicting roles as a static fact about the schema) but is not evaluated against any real configuration payload — see GRF-RFC-0043's known limitations for what this does and does not guarantee.

schemaV2Extensions.description: The complete v2 extension document for one dsconfig Schema document, keyed by that schema's field ids. A schemaV2Extensions document with both scopeDefs and fields absent or empty describes no v2 capability at all and is equivalent, in effect, to a plugin having no v2 extensions whatsoever — the companion v1 schema document behaves exactly as it always has.

scopeDefs.description: Every named scope this plugin's fields may belong to. Absent or empty means this plugin has no multi-connection needs; every field is then implicitly shared across the document's single, implicit scope, exactly as in v1. Every id in this array must be unique.

fields.description: Maps a field's id (matching a configField.id in the companion v1 Schema document) to that field's fieldExtensionV2. A field id with no entry here has no scopes, no role, no roleConflicts, and (if it is an item field) no pairRole.
